title: Otis and the Case of the Missing Rainbow
synopsis: Young detective Otis investigates why the rainbow disappeared from his neighborhood with help from his diverse group of friends, discovering the science behind rainbows along the way.

pages.text: Otis loved rainbows. Every day after school, he looked for them in the sky. Today was different. The rainbow was gone! "Where did it go?" Otis asked his mom. "I don't know," she said. "Maybe you can solve this mystery."

illustration.description: A young Black boy with curly hair and big curious eyes stands in a backyard, pointing up at a gray cloudy sky with a confused expression. His mother, a woman with natural hair in a colorful dress, stands nearby with a gentle smile. The yard has green grass and a wooden fence.
illustration.alt: Otis pointing at the sky while his mother watches

pages.text: Otis put on his detective hat. It was red with a big magnifying glass pin. "I will find that rainbow!" he said. He grabbed his notebook and ran outside. The mystery was about to begin.

illustration.description: Otis wearing a bright red detective hat with a shiny magnifying glass pin, holding a small blue notebook with determination. His eyes are wide and excited. He's standing by a colorful front door with flowering bushes nearby in vibrant purples and pinks.
illustration.alt: Otis in his detective hat holding a notebook

pages.text: First, Otis went to see his friend Luna. She had long black hair and loved science. "Luna!" he called. "The rainbow is missing! Will you help me find it?" Luna smiled big. "Yes! Let's solve this mystery together."

illustration.description: A Latina girl with long straight black hair and bright brown eyes stands on her porch steps, wearing a yellow t-shirt with a science beaker design. She's smiling excitedly at Otis. Behind her is a blue house with potted plants and colorful flowers in reds and oranges.
illustration.alt: Luna on her porch talking to Otis

pages.text: Otis and Luna walked around the neighborhood. They met their friend Kai at the park. Kai had short blonde hair and freckles. "What are you doing?" Kai asked. "Looking for the missing rainbow," said Otis. "Can you help us?"

illustration.description: Three children stand in a sunny park with green grass and tall trees. Kai, a child with short blonde hair and freckles wearing a green striped shirt, looks curious. Otis and Luna are explaining something with animated gestures. A playground with colorful equipment is visible in the background.
illustration.alt: Three friends talking in the park

pages.text: "I saw a rainbow yesterday," said Kai. "It was after the rain." Luna's eyes got big. "That's a clue! Rainbows need water and sun." Otis wrote in his notebook: RAINBOW NEEDS WATER AND SUN. "But where did the water go?" he wondered.

illustration.description: Luna with wide excited eyes pointing upward, as if explaining something important. Otis is writing carefully in his blue notebook with a focused expression. Kai listens with interest. The scene is bright with golden sunlight filtering through green leaves above them.
illustration.alt: Luna explaining while Otis takes notes

pages.text: The three friends looked up at the sky. It was sunny but no clouds anywhere. "No rain means no rainbow," said Luna. "But wait! I have an idea." She pointed to the sprinklers watering the grass. "Look! Tiny water drops in the air!"

illustration.description: The children looking up at a bright blue cloudless sky with their hands shading their eyes. Garden sprinklers spray arcs of water across green grass, creating tiny sparkles in the sunlight. The mood is hopeful and discovery-filled with warm golden and blue tones.
illustration.alt: Children observing sprinklers in the sunlight

pages.text: Luna ran to the sprinklers. "Stand here!" she called to Otis and Kai. They stood where the sun hit the water drops. Suddenly, colors appeared in the air! Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple! "The rainbow!" shouted Otis.

illustration.description: A beautiful rainbow arcs through the water spray from sprinklers as three children jump and cheer with pure joy. Otis has his arms raised in celebration, Luna claps her hands, and Kai points excitedly at the rainbow. The colors are vivid and magical against the bright sunny park setting.
illustration.alt: Children celebrating as they discover a rainbow in the sprinkler spray

pages.text: "We solved the mystery!" said Kai. "Rainbows aren't really missing," explained Luna. "They happen when sunlight goes through water drops. The light breaks into colors." Otis wrote: LIGHT + WATER = RAINBOW. "Science is so cool!" he said.

illustration.description: Otis writing triumphantly in his notebook while Luna gestures as if teaching, with a big smile. Kai nods along enthusiastically. Behind them, the rainbow continues to shimmer in the sprinkler mist. The scene is filled with warm, bright colors showing their happiness and accomplishment.
illustration.alt: Otis taking notes while Luna explains the science

pages.text: The three friends played in the rainbow all afternoon. They learned that rainbows are everywhere when you have sun and water. "Tomorrow let's look for more science mysteries," said Otis. "Yes!" said Luna and Kai together. Detective Otis was ready for his next case.

illustration.description: The three friends playing happily in the rainbow-filled sprinkler spray, running and dancing with pure joy. The rainbow colors swirl around them like magic. The setting sun casts a warm golden glow over the entire scene, creating a perfect ending mood of friendship and discovery.
illustration.alt: Three friends playing together in rainbow-colored water spray

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What does Otis wear when he becomes a detective?
choices: A red hat with a magnifying glass pin
questions.explanation: Otis puts on his red detective hat with a big magnifying glass pin when he decides to solve the mystery.

questions.question: What two things do rainbows need?
questions.explanation: Luna explains that rainbows need water and sun. The sunlight goes through water drops to create rainbow colors.

questions.question: Where do the friends finally find the rainbow?
choices: In the sprinklers at the park
questions.explanation: The friends discover a rainbow in the sprinkler spray when the sunlight hits the water drops.

questions.question: Who explains the science of rainbows?
questions.explanation: Luna, who loves science, explains that rainbows happen when sunlight goes through water drops and breaks into colors.

questions.question: How many colors are in the rainbow they find?
questions.explanation: The story mentions six colors in the rainbow: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple.

pages.title: Complete Detective Otis's Notes
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words from Otis's detective notebook!
pages.text: Today I solved the mystery of the missing ___1___. My friends Luna and ___2___ helped me. We learned that rainbows need ___3___ and sun. When sunlight goes through water drops, it breaks into ___4___. We found our rainbow in the park ___5___!

pages.title: Rainbow Science Facts
pages.instructions: Choose the right words to complete these science facts!
pages.text: Rainbows are made when ___1___ shines through tiny water ___2___. The light breaks apart into different ___3___. You can see rainbows after it ___4___ or near waterfalls and ___5___.
